## Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop

The User Tasks bar includes EZPay Cashless Drop tasks if the property has either EZ Pay
Smart Card or EZ Pay Mag Card functions available for players. These tasks involve
determining and reviewing summaries for the current accounting period, balancing batches,
and correcting variances. When reconciliation and/or acceptance of drop summaries matches
system totals, the status in the Period Timeline Ribbon changes to green.

The following tasks are available for completing the EZ Pay Cashless Drop portion of the audit:

· Import EZ Pay Smart Card Information-Imports the system WAT-in and system
WAT-out revenue summaries by machine.
OR

· Import EZ Pay Mag Card Information-Imports the system WAT-in and system WAT-
out revenue summaries by machine.
AND

· Audit EZ Pay Smart Card Meters-Audit and correct WAT-in and WAT-out variances
between actual and meters.

OR

Audit EZ Pay Mag Card Meters-Audit and correct WAT-in and WAT-out variances
between actual and meters.




![Figure 20-1 User Tasks List-EZ Pay Cashless Drop List](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/247.2.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="247" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop" -->


### 20.1 Retrieving EZ Pay Cashless Drops

The EZ Pay Cashless Drop is obtained from the EZ Pay end-of-day issuance unless configured
for drop-to-drop.


#### To retrieve the EZ Pay cashless drop:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop > Import Cashless Information on the User
Tasks list. The Retrieving Drop window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/248.1.png)




2\. Click Done when complete. The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/248.2.png)




### 20.2 WAT-In Variances

Navigate to Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meter > WAT In to audit incoming wager account
transfers (WAT). This information is obtained from the EZ Pay end-of-day issuance, unless
configured drop-to-drop.


#### 20.2.1 Viewing Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters

To view audit EZ Pay cashless meters:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop> Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZPay Cashless Meters window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/248.3.png)




2\. Select WAT In. The audit EZ Pay cashless meters for WAT in appear.

3\. Click All Dropped or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT in meters.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="248" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop" -->


#### 20.2.2 Editing EZ Pay Cashless System Amounts

Use the EZ Pay Cashless System WAT In Edit window to adjust system amounts and counts
for WAT in.

To edit EZ Pay cashless meters:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop> Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.

2\. Select WAT In. The audit EZ Pay cashless meters for WAT in appear.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT in meters.

4\. Click System. The EZ Pay Cashless System WAT In Edit window appears.

5\. Enter an amount in the System Amount and System Count fields.

6\. Click OK. The EZ Pay cashless meters meter is updated.


#### 20.2.3 Adjusting WAT-In Meter Variances

To adjust WAT-in meter variances:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop> Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.

2\. Select WAT In. The audit EZ Pay cashless meters for WAT in appear.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT in meters.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click Meters. The WAT In Meter Adjustment for Machine window appears.

6\. Enter the adjustment values for the Value Meter and Count Meter in the Adjustment
fields.

7\. Click OK. The adjustment is confirmed.


### 20.2.4 Accepting WAT-In Variances

To accept WAT-in variances:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop> Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.

2\. Select WAT In. Audit EZ Pay cashless meters for WAT in appear.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT in meters.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click Accept if the variance is within established guidelines. A confirmation prompt
appears.

6\. Click Yes. The variance is accepted.


### 20.2.5 Unaccepting WAT In Variances

To unaccept a WAT-in variance:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop> Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="249" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop" -->

2\. Select WAT In. Audit EZ Pay cashless meters for WAT in appear.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT in meters.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click Unaccept if the variance is not within established guidelines. A confirmation
prompt appears.

6\. Click Yes. The variance is no longer accepted.


#### 20.2.6 Entering WAT-In Comments

Adjustments to a WAT-in meter value may be documented with a comment explaining the
reason why.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/250.1.png)




Ì
Entering a reason is recommended throughout Machine Accounting.


##### To add comments to a WAT in adjustment:

1\. Select a machine and click Comment in the Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window. The
Machine Comment window opens.See WAT-In Variances on page 248




![Figure 20-2 Machine Comment](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/250.2.png)




2\. Click the Comment drop-down, then select a comment or enter a new comment.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/250.3.png)




İ
Select Active Comment if the comment will be used repeatedly. Select Advanced to
configure active and inactive comments.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="250" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop" -->




![Figure 20-3 Comment Status](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/251.1.png)




3\. Click OK to post the comment.
OR

Click Cancel to close the Comment Status window without the comment saving.


### 20.3 WAT-Out Variances

Navigate to Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters > WAT Out to audit outgoing wager account
transfers (WAT). Obtain this information from the EZ Pay end-of-day issuance.


#### 20.3.1 Viewing Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters

To view the Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop > Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/251.2.png)




### 2. Select WAT Out.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT out meters.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="251" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop" -->


#### 20.3.2 Editing EZ Pay Cashless System Amounts

Use the EZ Pay Cashless System WAT Out Edit window to make adjustments to the system
amounts and counts for WAT out.


##### To edit EZ Pay cashless meters:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop > Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.

2\. Select WAT Out.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT-out meters.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click System. The EZ Pay Cashless System WAT Out Edit window appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/252.1.png)




6\. Enter an amount in the System Amount and System Count fields.

7\. Click OK. to confirm.


#### 20.3.3 Adjusting WAT-Out Meter Variances

To adjust WAT-out meter variances:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop > Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.

2\. Select WAT Out.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT out meters.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click Meters. The WAT Out Meter Adjustment for Machine appears.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/252.2.png)




6\. Enter values in the Value Meter Adjustment Adjustment and Count Meter Adjustment
fields.

7\. Click OK. The adjustment is confirmed.

<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="252" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop" -->


#### 20.3.4 Accepting WAT-Out Variances

To accept WAT-out variances:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop > Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.

2\. Select WAT Out.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT-out meters.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click Accept if the variance is within established guidelines. A confirmation prompt
appears.

6\. Click Yes. The variance is accepted.


#### 20.3.5 Unaccepting WAT-Out Variances

To unaccept a WAT-out variance:

1\. Navigate to EZ Pay Cashless Drop > Audit Cashless Meters on the User Tasks list.
The Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window appears.

2\. Select WAT Out.

3\. Select All Machines or Exceptions to filter the display of WAT out meters.

4\. Select a machine.

5\. Click Unaccept if the variance is not within established guidelines. A confirmation
prompt appears.

6\. Click Yes. The variance is no longer accepted.


## 20.3.6 Entering WAT-Out Comments

Adjustments to a WAT-out meter value may be documented with a comment explaining the
reason why.


### To add a comment to a WAT-out adjustment:




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/253.1.png)




Entering a reason is recommended throughout Machine Accounting.

1\. Navigate to the Audit EZ Pay Cashless Meters window, then select a machine and click
Comment. The Machine Comment window opensSee WAT-Out Variances on page 251




![Figure 20-4 Machine Comment](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/253.2.png)




<!-- PageFooter="Machine Accounting 9.7.5" -->
<!-- PageNumber="253" -->
<!-- PageBreak -->

<!-- PageHeader="Chapter 20 EZ Pay Cashless Drop" -->

2\. Click the Comment drop-down, then select a comment or enter a new comment.




![](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/254.1.png)




Select Active Comment if the comment will be used repeatedly. Click Advanced to
configure active and inactive comments.




![Figure 20-5 Comment Status](MachineAccounting_UserGuide_images/254.2.png)




3\. Click OK. The comment is added.
OR
Click Cancel. The Comment Status window closes without the comment saving.
